vulnerability.reference: Lack of clinical validation across diverse patient populations and opaque policy decisions.
vulnerability.generated: Clinicians lack real-time signals to detect when shared-parameter RL models generate erroneous recommendations under patient variability.

impact.reference: Patient harm from inappropriate treatments and erosion of trust in AI clinical support.
impact.generated: Patients suffer incorrect treatments causing preventable complications, while institutional trust in AI decision support erodes.

dose_response.reference: Gradient conflicts from shared parameters enable extrapolation errors, which when deploying the policy in healthcare treatment recommendations exposes clinicians using decision support, leading to patient harm and erosion of trust.
dose_response.generated: Gradient conflicts from shared parameters amplify extrapolation errors, and through clinician use of decision support, the lack of real-time error-detection signals under patient variability leads to incorrect treatments, preventable complications, and eroded institutional trust.
